channel.title: Incorrect-Value on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Incorrect-Value on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] SQL Data Truncation Error Fix
item.description:
SQL Data Truncation Error Fix

This error occurs when a value being inserted or updated is too large for the column or doesn’t match the expected format. The message reads: Data truncation: Incorrect ... value or Data truncation: Data too long for column 'X'.

Description

Column definitions include size limits (VARCHAR length, INT range) and format requirements (DATE, DATETIME). When a value exceeds these constraints, the database rejects it. In strict SQL mode, this is an error; in lenient mode, the value is silently truncated.

item.title: [Solution] SQL Type Mismatch / Incorrect Value Error Fix
item.description:
SQL Type Mismatch / Incorrect Value Error Fix

This error occurs when a value doesn’t match the expected data type of the target column. The message reads: Incorrect datetime value, Incorrect ... value for column 'X', or Truncated incorrect ... value.

Description

SQL columns have strict data types. When inserting or updating, the database attempts to cast the provided value to the column’s type. If the cast fails — e.g., a string like 'abc' into a DATE column — the operation is rejected.
